query: How frequently does the US CPSC publish computer-related recalls? Looking at CPSC.gov and SaferProducts.gov recall history for the category 'computers' - approximately how many recalls per year, and are there typically gaps of 2 months with no computer recalls? List recent computer-related recalls in 2024, 2025 and 2026 with dates (including laptops, power supplies, monitors, batteries, chargers).
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Frequency of CPSC computer-related recalls:**

The most important finding is structural: **CPSC's literal "Computers" product category is essentially empty** — the SaferProducts.gov API returns an empty array for `ProductType=Computers` from 2024 onward. Computer-related recalls are instead filed under adjacent categories (power banks, chargers, AC adapters, batteries).

This creates two very different answers depending on definition:

- **Narrow (laptops, AC adapters, PC power supplies):** Only ~1–3 recalls/year. **Gaps of 2+ months — even 1–2+ years — are completely normal.** For instance, there was a ~26-month gap between the Dynabook/Toshiba laptop-adapter recall (Feb 2024) and the Apex Gaming ATX power-supply recall (Apr 2026).
- **Broad (adding power banks, chargers, lithium batteries):** ~15–25+ recalls/year — roughly one every 4–6 weeks in 2025. At this breadth, **2-month gaps are rare.**

**Recent recalls listed with dates (2024–2026)** include the flagship laptop-hardware recalls — Dynabook/Toshiba 15.5M AC adapters (2024-02-21) and Apex Gaming ATX PC power supplies (2026-04-16, ~17,730 units) — plus a steady cadence of power-bank/charger recalls (Anker >1M units 2025-06-12, Belkin, INIU, ESR HaloLock, Casely, IKEA VARMFRONT, Baseus, HTRC chargers, etc.).

**Bottom line on the gap question:** If a monitor watches only the exact "computers" category, 2-month gaps are expected and routine. If it watches the broader charger/battery/power-bank universe, recalls arrive roughly monthly. Full sourced data has been provided in the structured output.

sources.title: SaferProducts.gov REST Web Service — power bank recalls since 2024
sources.snippet: Lists power-bank recalls 2024-2026 including Anker, Belkin, INIU, ESR, Casely, IKEA VARMFRONT, Baseus, etc., with recall dates.

sources.title: SaferProducts.gov REST Web Service — battery charger recalls since 2024
sources.snippet: HTRC/Haisito T400 (2026-04-02), CT-ENERGY (2025-08-28), HTRC C240 (2024-12-12), Belkin smartwatch chargers (2024-12-05).

sources.title: SaferProducts.gov REST Web Service — ProductType=Computers (empty result)
sources.snippet: Returns empty array [] — no recalls classified under 'Computers' product type since 2024-01-01.

sources.title: SaferProducts.gov REST Web Service — laptop recall history
sources.snippet: Historical laptop recalls: Dynabook/Toshiba AC adapters 2024-02-21, Lenovo USB-C power banks 2023-11-22, Apple MacBook Pro 2019, Lenovo ThinkPad 2018, Sony VAIO, Toshiba, Panasonic battery packs 2016-2017.

sources.title: Apex Gaming PCs Recalls Manik and Apex-branded ATX Computer Power Supplies
sources.snippet: Recall Date: April 16, 2026. Units: about 17,730 (plus about 500 in Canada). Electrical shock/electrocution; missing on-product safety label.

sources.title: Dynabook Americas Recalls 15.5 Million Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters
sources.snippet: Announced February 21, 2024. AC adapters bundled with or sold separately for Toshiba-brand laptops; burn/fire hazard.

sources.title: Apex Gaming PCs recalls nearly 18,000 power supplies over missing safety labels
sources.snippet: CPSC issued recall on April 16 for approximately 17,730 Manik and Apex-branded ATX power supplies.

sources.title: ATX Computer Power Supplies recalled due to electrical hazard (Health Canada)
sources.snippet: Recall date 2026-04-16; Apex/Manik ATX power supplies; electrical hazard.

sources.title: CPSC Posts Product Recalls and Product Safety Warnings (April 16, 2026)
sources.snippet: April 16, 2026 CPSC recall roundup including Casely power-bank re-announcement after a fatality.

key_facts: CPSC/SaferProducts.gov API returns an empty array for ProductType=Computers for 2024 onward — the literal 'Computers' recall category is essentially unused.
Computer-related recalls are filed under adjacent categories: power banks/chargers (largest volume), battery chargers, AC adapters, laptop computers (rare), computer power supplies (rare).
Narrow computer/laptop-hardware definition: only ~1-3 recalls per year; gaps of 2+ months (often much longer) with no recall are normal.
Between the Feb 2024 Dynabook laptop-adapter recall and the April 2026 Apex Gaming PC power-supply recall there were no clear PC-hardware recalls — a gap over two years at the strict definition.
Broad computer-electronics definition (incl. power banks, chargers, batteries): ~15-25+ recalls per year; 2-month gaps are rare.
Power-bank recalls ran roughly one every 4-6 weeks through 2025.
Dynabook recalled 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters on 2024-02-21 (burn/fire hazard) — the flagship 2024 laptop-hardware recall.
Apex Gaming PCs recalled ~17,730 Manik/Apex ATX computer power supplies on 2026-04-16 (electrocution/missing safety label) — the clearest 2026 PC-hardware recall.
Anker recalled more than 1 million power banks on 2025-06-12 (fire/burn).
Genuine laptop-computer recalls are rare; most 'laptop' recalls concern the battery, adapter, or power bank rather than the computer.

data_points.metric: Approx. narrow computer/laptop-hardware recalls per year
Approx. broad computer-electronics recalls per year (power banks, chargers, batteries)
Dynabook Toshiba laptop AC adapters recalled
Apex Gaming ATX computer power supplies recalled (US)
Gap between consecutive strict PC-hardware recalls (Dynabook Feb 2024 to Apex Apr 2026)
